---
title: "Reasoning, Verification, and Epistemology"
status: "conceptual"
last_updated: "2026-06-24"
primary_sources:
  - "spinoza"
  - "uat"
  - "coherence_exchange"
  - "verification_bandwidth"
  - "treellm"
  - "viea"
  - "spinoza_composer"
  - "aletheia"
evidence_level: "argument"
open_evidence_gaps:
  - "Assigned source texts have not yet been ingested or summarized."
  - "No Codex tests have been implemented or run."
  - "Chapter prose is a scaffold, not a completed manuscript draft."
---

# Reasoning, Verification, and Epistemology

## Chapter status

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Status | conceptual |
| Last updated | 2026-06-24 |
| Primary source records | `spinoza`, `uat`, `coherence_exchange`, `verification_bandwidth`, `treellm`, `viea`, `spinoza_composer`, `aletheia` |
| Evidence level | argument |
| Source ingestion state | Source records assigned; source texts not yet ingested. |
| Test state | Planned only; no tests have been run. |

## Drafting guardrail

This stub is derived from the handoff outline and source inventory only. It does not claim that the listed source documents have been ingested, summarized, or independently verified.

## Problem

The stack needs explicit claim states, belief revision, uncertainty, and verification loops rather than fluent generation alone.

## Why existing approaches are insufficient

Generated text can be coherent without being grounded, checked, calibrated, or fit for high-risk action.

## Core claim

[09-core, support: argument] Reasoning and verification should maintain claim records with evidence tiers, contradiction handling, and calibrated uncertainty.

## Mechanism

- Extract claims from plans, context, and outputs.
- Assign support states and evidence tiers.
- Revise beliefs when contradiction or stronger evidence appears.

## Interfaces

- VCM supplies provenance.
- Planning and execution request verification before high-risk steps.
- Evidence matrices expose support state to readers and systems.

## Invariants

- Unsupported claims remain labeled.
- Contradictions are tracked instead of silently overwritten.
- High-risk domains pay a verification tax.

## Failure modes

- False verification.
- Silence failure when evidence is insufficient.
- Evaluator or citation drift.

## Minimal implementation

A claim ledger with support states, source handles, uncertainty, contradiction links, and review status.

## Codex test plan

| Test | Purpose | Status |
|---|---|---|
| Claim extraction test | Support or falsify this chapter's layer claim. | planned; not run |
| Contradiction detection test | Support or falsify this chapter's layer claim. | planned; not run |
| Belief revision test | Support or falsify this chapter's layer claim. | planned; not run |
| Evidence tier assignment test | Support or falsify this chapter's layer claim. | planned; not run |
| Uncertainty calibration test | Support or falsify this chapter's layer claim. | planned; not run |
| Insufficient-evidence silence test | Support or falsify this chapter's layer claim. | planned; not run |

## Source crosswalk

| Source ID | Title | Planned use |
|---|---|---|
| `spinoza` | Proof of Belief / The Spinoza Architecture | Planned use from inventory/handoff: Neurosymbolic belief, transparent axiomatic AI belief systems, verification, belief revision. |
| `uat` | Unified Adaptive Tribunal | Planned use from inventory/handoff: Multi-AI collaborative refinement/evaluation protocol. |
| `coherence_exchange` | The Coherence Exchange | Planned use from inventory/handoff: Found in AI generated paper dump. Use carefully; speculative synthesis of PlanForge, Spinoza, Talos, UAT, Alignment Field. |
| `verification_bandwidth` | Verification Bandwidth in Bounded Contexts | Planned use from inventory/handoff: Generation vs verification; long context is not automatically long-range reasoning. |
| `treellm` | TreeLLM | Planned use from inventory/handoff: Hierarchical semantic token system for grounded, efficient, explainable language modeling. |
| `viea` | Verified Intent-to-Execution Architecture | Planned use from inventory/handoff: Keystone source. Human intent -> command contracts -> artifacts -> routing -> runtime targets -> verification -> deployment -> feedback. |
| `spinoza_composer` | Spinoza Composer / Spinoza Trinity | Planned use from inventory/handoff: Narrative/compliance operating system variant. Use as applied Spinoza. |
| `aletheia` | Aletheia Foundry | Planned use from inventory/handoff: Safe general intelligence predecessor. Use ideas; avoid final branding if collision remains. |

## Summary

This chapter currently establishes the structural slot for this layer of the ASI stack. The next drafting pass should ingest the assigned sources, separate source-derived claims from design hypotheses, and update Appendix C when support states change.
